mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of the Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Vultr's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated Vultr features, but it may incur costs based on resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Vultr CLI, which is necessary for managing your Vultr resources from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr-cli /usr/local/bin/

vultr-cli version

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --region 'ewr' --version '1.30' --node-count 3 --plan 'vc2-2c-4gb'

```

> Replace 'ewr' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID> > ~/.kube/config

```

> Replace <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID> with your actual cluster ID.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see your nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up additional features like autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vultr/vultr-cloud-controller-manager/master/docs/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the cluster autoscaler.
> Deploy monitoring tools.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete --cluster-id <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID>

```

> Replace <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers or DNS records are cleaned up.

```bash

vultr-cli loadbalancer list

vultr-cli dns list

```

> Check for any remaining load balancers.
> Check for any remaining DNS records.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up your data, upgrading the cluster, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml

vultr-cli k8s upgrade --cluster-id <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID> --version '1.31'

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup your current cluster state.
> Upgrade to the desired Kubernetes version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your kubeconfig file is correctly configured with the right cluster context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the requested resources exceed the available capacity in the selected region.

**Fix:**
Try reducing the node count or selecting a different plan.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'LoadBalancer service is not working'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager is not deployed correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify that the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager is running and configured properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the node is not properly initialized or has failed to join the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs for errors and ensure that the node is correctly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.30, improved load balancer management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
